hook: You already interpret charts, averages, and scores in everyday life — from comparing phone prices to reading test results. But raw numbers alone can be misleading, and without the right tools you might miss the real story hiding in the data. How do statisticians turn a messy pile of numbers into clear, honest insights?
concept: This unit is about organizing and summarizing data so that patterns become visible and comparisons become meaningful — the formal idea is exploratory data analysis, the foundation of all statistical thinking.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to distinguish between categorical and numerical variables so you always choose the right tool for the right kind of data.
Master building frequency tables and histograms to reveal the overall shape and distribution of a dataset at a glance.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Measures of Center: Mean, Median, Mode
topic_roadmap.description: Apply the mean, median, and mode to describe where data tends to cluster, and recognize when each measure best represents a dataset.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Measures of Spread: Range, IQR, Standard Deviation
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to quantify how spread out data values are using the range, interquartile range $(	ext{IQR})$, and standard deviation $s$.
Construct box plots from the five-number summary $(	ext{min},\, Q_1,\, 	ext{median},\, Q_3,\, 	ext{max})$ to compare distributions visually and efficiently.
You will learn to identify outliers using the $1.5 	imes 	ext{IQR}$ rule and understand why resistant measures like the median stay reliable even when extreme values are present.
